scope_note: First conservative structured extraction from the canonical 2025 standards. Standard 3 is detailed; Standard 1 and Standard 4 are indexed for question-generation relevance; Standard 5 is high-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Recognizes Torah as לשון הקודש, learns examples of לשון נקיה, and develops awareness that anthropomorphic Torah language is not literal.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Important teacher framing, but not a first-pass discrete question lane.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Conceptually important, but not a simple diagnostic target without teacher mediation.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Builds knowledge of כתב אשורית, unusual letters, mesorah-based ניקוד, כתיב מלא/חסר, and קרי וכתיב as reading conventions that affect text study.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Relevant to later text-reading and commentary skills after review.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Can support convention checks for older students after human review.

extracted_substandards.notes: Conservative summary from the extracted table.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Recognizes that טעמי המקרא belong to the mesorah and guide punctuation, tune, and pronunciation in the printed חומש.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Relevant to parsing and ניקוד work, but not yet question-ready.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Useful for advanced reading diagnostics after review.

extracted_substandards.notes: Appendix-linked details intentionally not fully structured here.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Learns how the Torah is divided into סדרות, פרשיות, and פרקים, and later uses פתוחות and סתומות to segment topics more meaningfully than chapter breaks alone.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Strongly related to navigation and segmentation, but still source-mode only.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Supports navigation and later passage segmentation tasks.

extracted_substandards.notes: Especially relevant for navigation and passage-division tooling.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Progresses from basic numerical values of Hebrew letters through extended gematria ranges, ending with calculation of phrases as well as words.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Helpful for navigation and later Rashi work, but not central to first Chumash diagnostics.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Discrete and testable, but lower priority than direct Chumash language skills.

extracted_substandards.notes: Kept in the index because it affects navigation and later commentary fluency.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Learns the sequence, spelling, standard and alternate names, and major themes of the five חומשים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Orientation-relevant, but not a core language-skill lane.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Foundational orientation skill rather than a high-value diagnostic language target.

extracted_substandards.notes: Indexed because it affects text navigation and curricular sequencing.

extracted_substandards.standard_title: The Names of the סדרות / פרשיות
extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Builds sequencing, matching, spelling, and content-clue knowledge for פרשיות / סדרות across the five חומשים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Navigation-relevant, but outside the first language-skill extraction focus.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Primarily orientation and sequencing rather than text-analysis skill.

extracted_substandards.notes: Useful for curriculum orientation and later navigation tooling.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Locates ספר, פרשה, פרק, פסוק, עליות, פתוחות/סתומות, רש"י, and אונקלוס, with later movement into other מפרשים used in class.
prerequisite_skills: 1.09 The Names of the סדרות / פרשיות

question_generation_relevance.notes: One of the most directly reusable Standard 1 areas for later diagnostic navigation tasks.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Can support navigation diagnostics and later commentary-prep checks.

extracted_substandards.notes: Directly relevant to navigation and teacher-only study fluency checks.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Builds a cumulative noun vocabulary, then uses previously learned nouns for contextual translation and multiple-meaning choices.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Highly relevant to future diagnostic and question-generation lanes.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Directly assessable through word meaning, contextual meaning, and transfer into unlearned פסוקים.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Builds cumulative root vocabulary for verbs, then identifies weak-letter and altered-root patterns and chooses contextual translations.

question_generation_relevance.notes: One of the clearest future rule-to-question bridges in the standards.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Root meaning and root-identification skills are central to later independent Chumash reading.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Builds a cumulative bank of function words and chooses contextual meanings for words with multiple uses.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Very relevant to later direct translation and lane-routing decisions.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Function-word confusion is a strong diagnostic signal in Chumash reading.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Moves from identifying nouns and adjectives in learned words to recognizing gender, number, irregular forms, and סמיכות in unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Strong candidate for future diagnostic grammar lanes.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Concrete, teachable, and directly connected to Chumash translation accuracy.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Progresses from basic English and Hebrew pronoun categories to identifying referents, suffixes, and embedded pronouns in learned and unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Pronoun referent work is highly relevant to generated-question QA and parsing.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Pronoun referent confusion is a high-value diagnostic signal in פסוק understanding.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.standard_title: Prepositions, Conjunctions and the Definite Article
extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Builds from direct recognition of conjunctions and articles to distinguishing multiple prefix functions, article types, and the two meanings of את in learned and unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: A very strong future diagnostic lane because the standards name discrete, testable forms.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Misreading prefixes and function words is a central source of Chumash translation error.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Moves from identifying verbs and simple tense markers to translating verbs across person, tense, active/passive forms, and binyan families.

question_generation_relevance.notes: One of the richest future diagnostic/question-generation standards, but it must remain human-reviewed.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Verb parsing and tense/binyan errors are central to independent text comprehension.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Groups a פסוק into meaningful phrases and adjusts Hebrew word order when translating into English.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Excellent future source for syntax-aware translation prompts and QA.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Grouping and word-order mistakes are highly visible in student translations.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Uses טעמי המקרא to divide, punctuate, and translate a פסוק appropriately.

question_generation_relevance.notes: A strong future teacher-reviewed lane for advanced parsing tasks.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Parsing with טעמים directly affects translation and comprehension quality.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: For advanced students, explains the systems of תנועות, שבאים, דגשים, syllables, and the interaction between ניקוד and טעמי המקרא.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Highly relevant for advanced grammar and later Rashi work, but too sensitive for unreviewed question generation.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Powerful for advanced students, but intentionally gated because the extracted table is compact and advanced.

extracted_substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Progresses from retelling the 5 W’s after teaching to answering them independently in new פסוקים using language skills and context clues, while answering בלשון המקרא.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Directly relevant to future comprehension item generation.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: A strong indicator of whether students can pull explicit information from text.

extracted_substandards.notes: One of the clearest bridges from standards language into future question-generation relevance.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Moves from distinguishing narrative and quote to locating quote boundaries, including nested quotes, in learned and then unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Strong candidate for targeted comprehension checks.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Quote-boundary confusion often exposes shallow narrative tracking.

extracted_substandards.notes: The quote-within-quote progression is explicit enough for indexing.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Sequences events chronologically and then sequences cause and effect, first in translated learned texts and later in unlearned פסוקים using language skills and context clues.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Very relevant to comprehension and logic-question families.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Sequencing and cause-effect are core transferable comprehension skills.

extracted_substandards.notes: Clear Higher Order Thinking progression.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Compares and contrasts personalities, events, and texts, moving from teacher-pointed comparisons to independent work with unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Directly relevant to later passage-comparison items and teacher-reviewed tasks.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Powerful but usually requires more text and careful scoring design.

extracted_substandards.notes: Useful for future richer assessment design, but not a first micro-question lane.

extracted_substandards.standard_title: Dividing Into Segments and Finding the Main Ideas
extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Divides פסוקים and larger ענינים into segments, identifies themes and main ideas, and summarizes by distinguishing main ideas from details.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Strong relevance for later comprehension, though some tasks may be open-ended.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Main-idea and segmentation ability are major indicators of real comprehension.

extracted_substandards.notes: Especially relevant to future passage-comprehension scaffolds and summary tasks.

extracted_substandards.standard_title: Noticing Anomalies and Deviations in the Text
extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Moves from recognizing repetition and other anomalies pointed out by a teacher to identifying missing subjects, missing objects, extra letters, grammatical inconsistencies, and narrative/logical difficulties independently.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Important for advanced source-sensitive question design and later Rashi prep.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Valuable, but may require careful human review to avoid over-inference.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-value but riskier; should stay human-reviewed before generated use.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Identifies figurative language, anthropomorphic language, and the significance of contextual meaning in פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Directly relevant to contextual-meaning assessment, but requires careful source review.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Important and teachable, but easily overreaches without careful review.

extracted_substandards.notes: Potentially useful for later contextual-meaning lanes, but must stay source-conservative.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Combines ideas and information from texts, commentaries, and later personal research into new communications that show comprehension, analysis, and synthesis.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Relevant to richer performance tasks, but not a first automatic question lane.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Highly valuable, though usually open-ended and teacher-reviewed.

extracted_substandards.notes: Kept as structured relevance, not as a current question template.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Learns to identify, transcribe, and read רש"י script with and without נקודות.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Future upper-grade relevance, but intentionally indexed only in this branch.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Useful for later readiness checks, not current Chumash question generation.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Deciphers and translates common רש"י abbreviations appearing in learned commentaries.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Relevant later for commentary access, but not extracted in full detail here.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Important for Rashi readiness, but outside the current main extraction focus.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Translates vocabulary commonly used in רש"י from the attached Appendix when it appears in learned רש"י.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Relevant later for Rashi fluency, but not a current extraction focus.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Teacher-facing readiness skill at this stage.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Groups and translates רש"י, moving from teacher-supported grouping to independent work with more complex commentaries.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Potentially relevant later, but out of scope for this first structured standards pass.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Deferred until a fuller Rashi extraction branch.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Explains רש"י’s question and answer, uses the דיבור המתחיל to identify what is bothering רש"י, and categorizes the type of commentary question.
prerequisite_skills: 4.06 Noticing Anomalies and Deviations in the Text

question_generation_relevance.notes: High future value, but only indexed here because a full Rashi extraction would require a dedicated review pass.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Important for upper-grade commentary study, but not current runtime scope.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Restates and summarizes רש"י in one’s own words and explains the פסוק according to רש"י.

question_generation_relevance.notes: More performance-based and teacher-reviewed than first-pass question-generation ready.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Useful later for deeper commentary comprehension checks.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Divides רש"י into components such as דיבור המתחיל, question, answer, and supporting evidence.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Potentially useful for teacher-review workflows, but deliberately not expanded here.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Teacher-facing and upper-grade only for now.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.

extracted_substandards.student_expectation: Explains the role and style of מפרשי רש"י, navigates from רש"י to related מפרשים, and explains רש"י according to those commentators.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Valuable later, but intentionally kept at index-only level in this branch.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Out of current extraction scope except as a roadmap record.

extracted_substandards.notes: High-level index only.
